questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: Speed up to clear the intersection
The light is about to turn red; stop if you can do so safely
Stop and wait for a green arrow
The signal is broken; treat it as a stop sign
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that the signal is changing to red, so you should stop if it is safe to do so.

questions.prompt: What does a flashing red traffic light require you to do?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Stop completely, then proceed when it is safe
Stop and remain stopped until it turns green
questions.explanation: A flashing red light has the same meaning as a stop sign: come to a full stop, then go when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What is the shape of a stop sign?
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always a red octagon, a shape used for no other type of sign so it can be recognized instantly.

questions.prompt: A solid yellow line on your side of the center of the road means:
choices: You may pass when no traffic is coming
Passing is not allowed on your side
questions.explanation: A solid yellow center line on your side means passing is prohibited there because it is unsafe to do so.

questions.prompt: What color are most warning signs, such as those marking a curve or a hazard ahead?
questions.explanation: Warning signs are typically yellow with black symbols or letters to alert drivers to hazards ahead.

questions.prompt: What does a downward-pointing triangle sign mean?
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is a yield sign, meaning you must slow down and give the right-of-way to other traffic.

questions.prompt: What color are signs used in highway construction and maintenance work zones?
questions.explanation: Construction and work-zone signs are orange to warn drivers of changed conditions and the presence of workers.

questions.prompt: A broken yellow line in the center of a two-way road means:
choices: Passing is permitted when it is safe
The lanes move in the same direction
questions.explanation: A broken yellow line indicates that passing is allowed on that side when the road ahead is clear and it is safe.

questions.prompt: What does a green traffic light mean?
choices: Go if the intersection is clear, after yielding to any traffic and pedestrians already in it
Speed up before the light changes
questions.explanation: A green light means you may proceed, but you must first yield to any vehicles or pedestrians still in the intersection.

questions.prompt: White lane lines on a roadway separate:
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
The road from the shoulder only
questions.explanation: White lines separate lanes of traffic traveling in the same direction, while yellow lines separate opposing traffic.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, two vehicles arrive at exactly the same time. Who has the right-of-way?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach a four-way stop at the same time, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you are making a left turn at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: Oncoming traffic and pedestrians in the crosswalk
No one, because you turn first
questions.explanation: A driver turning left must yield to oncoming traffic and to pedestrians crossing the road being entered.

questions.prompt: A pedestrian is crossing in a crosswalk in front of you. You should:
choices: Yield and let the pedestrian cross safely
Proceed because vehicles always have priority
questions.explanation: Drivers must yield the right-of-way to pedestrians in marked and unmarked crosswalks.

questions.prompt: An emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on. What should you do?
choices: Pull over to the right edge of the road and stop
Speed up to get out of the way
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling to the right edge of the roadway and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: At an intersection with no signs or signals, who must yield?
choices: The driver who arrives later or, if arriving together, the driver on the left
The driver on the right always
questions.explanation: At an uncontrolled intersection, you yield to vehicles already there, and if you arrive together you yield to the driver on your right.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you signal your intention?
choices: Right as you begin the turn
Only if other cars are nearby
questions.explanation: Signaling at least 100 feet ahead gives other drivers and pedestrians time to react to your turn.

questions.prompt: You approach a pedestrian using a white cane or a guide dog. You must:
choices: Sound your horn to warn them
questions.explanation: Drivers must stop and yield to pedestrians who are blind and using a white cane or guide dog.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the circle
questions.explanation: Traffic already circulating in a roundabout has the right-of-way, so entering drivers must yield.

questions.prompt: From which lane should you begin a right turn?
choices: From the lane closest to the right curb
From the center of the road
questions.explanation: Right turns should be made from the lane nearest the right curb, turning into the nearest right lane.

questions.prompt: A vehicle is already stopped at a stop sign at a four-way stop when you arrive. You should:
choices: Let that vehicle go first, then proceed when it is your turn
Go first because you are moving
questions.explanation: At a four-way stop the first vehicle to arrive and stop has the right-of-way to proceed first.

questions.prompt: In Wisconsin, what is the default speed limit on a road where no other limit is posted?
questions.explanation: Where no other speed limit is posted, Wisconsin's default maximum speed limit is 55 mph.

questions.prompt: What is the typical maximum speed limit on Wisconsin freeways and interstate highways?
questions.explanation: Wisconsin allows speeds up to 70 mph on freeways and interstate highways where posted.

questions.prompt: What is the usual speed limit in urban and residential districts in Wisconsin?
questions.explanation: The standard speed limit in Wisconsin's urban and residential districts is 25 mph unless otherwise posted.

questions.prompt: What following distance is generally recommended behind the vehicle ahead in good conditions?
choices: At least three to four seconds
questions.explanation: A following distance of at least three to four seconds gives you time to react and stop safely.

questions.prompt: When children are present in a Wisconsin school zone, the speed limit is commonly:
questions.explanation: School zones in Wisconsin often require slowing to 15 mph when children are present to protect them.

questions.prompt: You should increase your following distance when:
choices: The road is wet, icy, or visibility is poor
The road is dry and clear
questions.explanation: Slippery roads and poor visibility increase stopping distance, so you need extra space ahead.

questions.prompt: Even when the posted limit is higher, you must:
choices: Drive at a speed that is reasonable and safe for current conditions
Always drive the maximum posted limit
questions.explanation: Wisconsin law requires driving at a reasonable and prudent speed for conditions, which may be below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: Driving too slowly in normal traffic is dangerous because it:
choices: Blocks the normal flow and can cause crashes
Saves fuel and is always safest
questions.explanation: Driving much slower than surrounding traffic disrupts the flow and can lead to collisions.

questions.prompt: As your speed increases, the distance needed to stop your vehicle:
questions.explanation: Higher speeds require much greater stopping distances because of increased momentum and reaction distance.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane highway, slower-moving traffic should:
questions.explanation: Slower traffic should keep right and leave the left lane open for passing.

questions.prompt: You may NOT pass another vehicle when:
choices: A solid yellow line is on your side of the center
A broken yellow line is on your side
The road is straight and clear
You are on a one-way street
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means passing is prohibited because sight distance or conditions make it unsafe.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, you should:
choices: Signal, check mirrors, and look over your shoulder for blind spots
questions.explanation: Signaling, checking mirrors, and looking over your shoulder ensures no vehicle is hidden in your blind spot.

questions.prompt: On a two-lane road, you should generally not pass within how many feet of an intersection or railroad crossing?
questions.explanation: Passing is prohibited within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad crossing because of limited sight distance and conflicting traffic.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, you should return to your lane when:
choices: You can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
questions.explanation: Returning only after you can see the passed vehicle in your mirror ensures you have enough room not to cut it off.

questions.prompt: On an undivided road, you approach a stopped school bus with flashing red lights from the opposite direction. You must:
choices: Stop until the red lights stop flashing
questions.explanation: On an undivided road, traffic in both directions must stop for a school bus with flashing red lights until they stop flashing.

questions.prompt: On a multi-lane road, the left lane is generally intended for:
questions.explanation: The left lane is primarily for passing; slower traffic should keep to the right.

questions.prompt: You should not attempt to pass when approaching:
choices: A hill or curve where you cannot see far enough ahead
A long straight stretch of clear road
questions.explanation: Passing on a hill or curve is dangerous because oncoming vehicles may be hidden from view.

questions.prompt: A solid white line between lanes going the same direction means:
choices: Lane changes are discouraged; stay in your lane
questions.explanation: A solid white line discourages crossing, signaling you should stay in your lane unless necessary.

questions.prompt: When being passed by another vehicle, you should:
choices: Maintain your speed and stay in your lane
questions.explanation: Keeping a steady speed and lane position lets the passing driver complete the pass safely.

questions.prompt: On a divided highway, when a school bus on the opposite side stops with flashing red lights, oncoming drivers separated by a median:
choices: Must stop until the lights stop flashing
questions.explanation: On a divided highway, only vehicles traveling in the same direction as the bus must stop, not those across a median.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist in Wisconsin, you must leave at least:
questions.explanation: Wisconsin law requires drivers to give at least three feet of clearance when passing a bicyclist.

questions.prompt: Under Wisconsin's Move Over Law, when you approach a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights and there are two lanes in your direction, you should:
choices: Move over into the farther lane if you can do so safely
questions.explanation: The Move Over Law requires changing to a non-adjacent lane when possible to give stopped emergency and service vehicles a safety zone.

questions.prompt: Why are motorcycles harder to see than cars?
choices: They are smaller and can be hidden in blind spots
questions.explanation: A motorcycle's small size makes it easy to overlook, so drivers must check blind spots carefully.

questions.prompt: If you cannot see a large truck's side mirrors, it means:
choices: The truck driver cannot see you
questions.explanation: Trucks have large blind spots; if you can't see the driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see your vehicle.

questions.prompt: A vehicle displaying a slow-moving orange triangle emblem is:
choices: Traveling much slower than normal traffic
questions.explanation: The orange triangle marks slow-moving vehicles like farm equipment, so approach with caution and be ready to slow down.

questions.prompt: Large trucks need more room than cars to:
questions.explanation: Because of their size and weight, trucks require longer stopping distances and wider turns.

questions.prompt: If you cannot safely change lanes for a stopped emergency vehicle, the Move Over Law requires you to:
choices: Slow down and pass with caution
questions.explanation: When you cannot move over safely, you must reduce speed and pass carefully to protect roadside workers and responders.

questions.prompt: When sharing the road with a bicyclist, you should treat the bicycle as:
choices: A vehicle with a right to use the road
Something that must always yield to cars
questions.explanation: Bicyclists have the legal right to use the road and must be given room and respect like other vehicles.

questions.prompt: When you want to pass a large truck, you should:
choices: Pass promptly on the left and avoid lingering in its blind spots
Stay beside it for a while
questions.explanation: Passing quickly on the left and not lingering beside a truck keeps you out of its large blind spots.

questions.prompt: In Wisconsin, the legal blood alcohol concentration limit for an adult driver age 21 or older is:
questions.explanation: It is illegal for adult drivers in Wisconsin to operate a vehicle with a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08 or higher.

questions.prompt: For drivers under age 21 in Wisconsin, the alcohol limit is:
choices: Zero tolerance — any measurable amount is illegal
questions.explanation: Wisconsin enforces a zero-tolerance law, so any measurable alcohol is a violation for drivers under 21.

questions.prompt: Texting while driving in Wisconsin is:
questions.explanation: Wisconsin law prohibits writing, sending, or reading text messages while driving for every driver.

questions.prompt: A driver with an instruction permit or probationary license may use a cell phone while driving:
questions.explanation: Permit and probationary drivers may not use a wireless phone while driving except to report an emergency.

questions.prompt: Alcohol affects your driving by:
choices: Slowing reaction time and impairing judgment
questions.explanation: Alcohol slows reactions, clouds judgment, and reduces coordination, all of which make driving dangerous.

questions.prompt: By driving on Wisconsin roads, you give 'implied consent' to:
choices: Chemical testing for alcohol or drugs when lawfully requested
questions.explanation: Implied consent means that by driving you agree to submit to chemical testing when an officer has probable cause.

questions.prompt: Cell phone use is prohibited for all drivers when:
choices: Driving in a construction zone where workers are present
questions.explanation: Wisconsin bans handheld cell phone use in marked construction or maintenance zones where workers are at risk, except to report an emergency.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a form of distracted driving?
choices: Taking your eyes, hands, or mind off the task of driving
Keeping both hands on the wheel
questions.explanation: Distraction is anything that takes your eyes, hands, or attention away from driving.

questions.prompt: The safest way to avoid impaired driving is to:
choices: Never drive after drinking alcohol or using drugs
questions.explanation: There is no safe way to drive while impaired; the only safe choice is to not drive after drinking or using drugs.

questions.prompt: In Wisconsin, you must turn on your headlights whenever:
choices: Your windshield wipers are in use
You are on a city street
questions.explanation: Wisconsin law requires headlights to be on any time you are using your windshield wipers.

questions.prompt: When driving in fog, you should use:
questions.explanation: Low beams cut through fog better, while high beams reflect off the fog and reduce visibility.

questions.prompt: Headlights are required during 'hours of darkness,' which begin:
questions.explanation: Hours of darkness run from one-half hour after sunset to one-half hour before sunrise, when headlights are required.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
questions.explanation: Easing off the accelerator and steering toward your intended path helps you regain control during a skid.

questions.prompt: Which road surfaces tend to freeze first in cold weather?
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses freeze before other roads because cold air circulates above and below them.

questions.prompt: On wet or icy roads, you should:
choices: Slow down and increase your following distance
questions.explanation: Reduced traction on wet or icy roads means you need lower speed and more space to stop safely.

questions.prompt: If visibility becomes so poor in a storm that you cannot drive safely, you should:
choices: Pull completely off the road and turn on your hazard lights
Speed up to get through it
questions.explanation: When you cannot see well enough to drive, pull fully off the road and use hazard lights until conditions improve.

questions.prompt: On slick roads, you should brake by:
choices: Applying the brakes gently and smoothly
questions.explanation: Gentle, smooth braking helps prevent skids on slippery surfaces.

questions.prompt: Wisconsin law also requires headlights to be on when:
choices: Weather conditions limit visibility, such as rain, fog, or snow
questions.explanation: Headlights must be used in adverse weather that reduces visibility so others can see you.

questions.prompt: You may not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant in Wisconsin?
questions.explanation: Parking within 15 feet of a fire hydrant is prohibited so firefighters have clear access.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Turning wheels toward the curb when facing downhill lets the curb stop the vehicle if it rolls.

questions.prompt: Who is required to wear a seat belt in a moving vehicle in Wisconsin?
questions.explanation: Wisconsin requires every driver and passenger to wear a properly fastened seat belt.

questions.prompt: Children must be secured in a child safety seat or booster based on their:
questions.explanation: Wisconsin's child restraint law bases the required seat on the child's age, height, and weight.

questions.prompt: When facing uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Facing uphill, turning wheels away from the curb lets the rear tire roll back against it if the vehicle moves.

questions.prompt: Which of these is an illegal place to park?
questions.explanation: Parking on a crosswalk or sidewalk blocks pedestrians and is prohibited.

questions.prompt: Before opening your car door into the street after parking, you should:
choices: Check for approaching traffic and bicycles
questions.explanation: Checking for traffic and bicyclists before opening your door prevents collisions and injuries.

questions.prompt: When parking on any hill, you should also:
choices: Leave the car in neutral with brake off
questions.explanation: Always set the parking brake on a hill so the vehicle cannot roll if the wheels alone do not hold it.

questions.prompt: Without a curb, when parking on a slope you should turn your wheels:
choices: Toward the edge of the road
Toward the center of the road
questions.explanation: With no curb, turning wheels toward the road's edge keeps a rolling vehicle from drifting into traffic.

questions.prompt: What is the minimum age to apply for an instruction permit in Wisconsin?
questions.explanation: Wisconsin allows applying for an instruction permit at age 15 years and 6 months.

questions.prompt: While driving with an instruction permit, you must be accompanied by:
choices: A licensed driver at least 21 years old seated beside you
No one if it is daytime
questions.explanation: An instruction-permit holder must always have a qualified licensed driver age 21 or older in the front passenger seat.

questions.prompt: How many hours of supervised driving practice must a Wisconsin teen complete before licensing?
choices: At least 30 hours, including 10 at night
questions.explanation: Wisconsin requires at least 30 hours of supervised practice, with at least 10 of those hours during darkness.

questions.prompt: The Wisconsin knowledge (written) test contains how many questions?
questions.explanation: The Wisconsin knowledge test consists of 50 questions covering road rules and signs.

questions.prompt: What percentage must you score to pass the Wisconsin knowledge test?
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 80 percent of the knowledge test questions correctly to pass.

questions.prompt: During the first nine months of holding a probationary license, a driver generally may carry:
choices: No more than one passenger under 25 who is not immediate family
questions.explanation: For the first nine months, probationary drivers are limited to one non-family passenger under age 25.

questions.prompt: A probationary driver is generally restricted from driving alone between:
questions.explanation: Probationary drivers face nighttime restrictions between midnight and 5:00 a.m., with limited exceptions for home, school, and work travel.

questions.prompt: Of the required supervised practice hours, how many must be at night?
questions.explanation: At least 10 of the 30 required supervised practice hours must be driven during hours of darkness.

questions.prompt: Before getting a probationary license, in addition to the knowledge test you must pass:
questions.explanation: New drivers must pass both the knowledge test and a behind-the-wheel road test to earn a probationary license.

questions.prompt: Wisconsin tracks traffic violations using a system based on:
questions.explanation: Wisconsin assigns demerit points for traffic convictions, and too many can lead to suspension.

questions.prompt: Accumulating how many demerit points within 12 months leads to suspension in Wisconsin?
questions.explanation: Reaching 12 or more demerit points within a 12-month period results in a license suspension.

questions.prompt: Refusing a lawfully requested chemical test for alcohol in Wisconsin results in:
questions.explanation: Under implied consent, refusing a chemical test brings license revocation separate from any OWI conviction.

questions.prompt: Violating restrictions on your probationary license can:
choices: Delay your progress toward a full license
questions.explanation: Breaking probationary-license rules can extend restrictions and delay earning an unrestricted license.

questions.prompt: Penalties are generally most severe for offenses such as:
questions.explanation: Serious offenses like impaired and reckless driving carry the heaviest penalties because of the danger they create.

questions.prompt: Wearing your seat belt is:
choices: Required by law for everyone in the vehicle
questions.explanation: Wisconsin's seat belt law requires every occupant of the vehicle to be buckled up.

questions.prompt: If you are convicted of a moving violation, points are:
questions.explanation: A conviction for a moving violation adds demerit points to your Wisconsin driving record.

questions.prompt: Leaving the scene of a crash you were involved in is:
choices: A serious offense with significant penalties
Allowed if no one was hurt
questions.explanation: Leaving the scene of a crash is a serious violation that carries significant legal penalties.

questions.prompt: Failing to obey the Move Over Law in Wisconsin can result in:
choices: A fine and possible suspension of driving privileges
questions.explanation: Violating the Move Over Law can bring fines and, in some cases, suspension of your operating privilege.
